{"step_by_step_thinking": "Randomization in a clinical trial is known to reduce confounding and decrease selection bias, ensuring that the two groups are comparable. However, randomization does not inherently increase the external validity of the trial. External validity refers to the extent to which the results of a study can be generalized to other settings, populations, or times. While randomization can help achieve balance between groups, it does not directly address the generalizability of the findings.", "answer_choice": "D"}
